what_it_does: Catalogs the Obsidian plugins already installed in the Obsidian Builder vault and the active Obsidian Base files, annotating each with its relevance to a board/address runtime.
observations: obsidian-local-rest-api exposes an HTTP read/write path on port 27124 TLS with an API key stored in data.json
obsidian-advanced-uri provides obsidian:// deep-links into the vault from an external surface
vi-chat is a custom plugin by Tim that adds a context-aware AI chat sidebar for vault operations
realclaudian / Claudian is a custom plugin that embeds Claude Code / Codex as collaborators inside the vault
.base files are in active use, including Dashboard.base and Spaces/Mobile Parity/02 Status Board.base
Status Board.base is an issue tracker that filters by note.type == issue and groups by severity/triage, resembling a board-as-vault implementation
standouts: obsidian-local-rest-api is flagged as a real second-writer channel the runtime could call
Tim's custom vi-chat and Claudian plugins mean AI/agent surfaces already run inside the vault
Status Board.base is presented as a working template for rendering board:// items as a native Base
conventions: Uses standard noticeboard item frontmatter with id, address, type, source, state, title, author_session, channel, and part_of link
Uses a markdown table to compare plugins by name, capability, and caveat
Uses a bullet list for Base files and a free-form paragraph for their significance
concerns: The REST API key is stored plaintext in obsidian-local-rest-api/data.json
The REST API TLS certificate is self-signed
A second-writer channel into the vault raises concurrency and trust questions not addressed here
notes: This item inventories Tim's Obsidian plugin loadout and active Base views, flagging the REST API, custom AI plugins, and an issue-tracking Base as ready-made infrastructure for a board runtime.
questions: Which exact endpoints and methods does obsidian-local-rest-api expose?
How does vi-chat differ from Claudian in terms of session model and vault mutations?
What is the mapping between board:// addresses and rows/records in a .base file?
Are plugin configuration files, including the REST API key, committed to the git repository?
What access controls or rate limits exist for the REST API?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose is to list plugins already installed and relevant Base files; the content does exactly that, with relevance annotations.
apparent_themes: Reuse existing Obsidian ecosystem rather than rebuild
Board-as-vault rendered through native database views
Human-AI collaboration surfaces embedded in the workspace
Second-writer channels and deep-link bridges between systems
intention_signals: Identify off-the-shelf and custom Obsidian capabilities that can serve as runtime substrate for an addressable board/ledger system without starting from scratch.
novelty: Treats an Obsidian vault as a live production environment with two custom AI plugins already embedded and a native Base issue tracker as a concrete, working precedent for board-like views.
contribution: Provides a plugin inventory with bite-sized relevance notes and points to a working Base issue tracker that demonstrates board-like views over vault files.
summary_for_embedding: A noticeboard item listing 17 installed Obsidian plugins (including local REST API, advanced URI, custom vi-chat and Claudian plugins) and two active Obsidian Base files, assessing their relevance to a board/address runtime.
intention_for_embedding: To surface existing Obsidian and plugin capabilities that could be reused as infrastructure for rendering and interacting with board items.
